rationale: The citing paper explicitly centers on transaction-cost contract design in multivendor multicomponent systems with constructs like modularity and unconstrained interbrand mix-and-match, which closely matches the cited paper’s transaction-cost framework for modular-system buying and single vs. multiple sourcing decisions. That conceptual and construct-level overlap suggests the 2003 paper could be a theory/mechanism foundation rather than a generic background citation, even though the raw evidence only confirms a DOI match in the references.
